title: rubus leucandrus var. procerus
intro: Rubus leucandrus var. procerus, also known as the dwarf raspberry or procerous raspberry, is a lesser-known variety of the Rubus leucandrus species, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and characterized by its compact growth and edible fruits. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Rubus leucandrus var. procerus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive well-being, among others. Herbal preparations of this plant may include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and wellness.
